rolling_30d.summary: myAutoLoan experienced significant MoM decline across core metrics: sessions dropped 68.4% to 10,994, engaged sessions fell 60.0% to 865, and engagement rate softened by 1.7 pp to 7.9%. However, YoY growth remains exceptional—sessions surged +1553.2% and engaged sessions grew +136.3%—reflecting strong Q1 2026 momentum (82,214 YTD sessions vs. 2,199 in 2025, +3638.7%). Direct traffic dominates at 96.9% of sessions but declined 68.9% MoM, signaling potential seasonal pullback or campaign pause. High-value conversion events show mixed signals: apply_now dropped 25.4% MoM but grew 12.3% YoY (91 events), while calls nearly flatlined at only 2 events. Navigational clicks surged 89.7% MoM to 129 events, indicating increased site exploration but lower-intent behavior. The homepage (/) remains the strongest performer with 39.3% engagement rate and 153 engaged sessions, though it declined 4.9% MoM. Geographic landing pages show emerging traction in New York and Michigan locations, though most are newly tracked with minimal volume.

quick_wins.title: Homepage remains engagement engine
quick_wins.detail: / landing page drove 389 sessions with 39.3% engagement rate (153 engaged sessions), substantially outperforming all other pages and declining only 4.9% MoM despite overall traffic drop.

quick_wins.title: Apply_now event shows YoY resilience
quick_wins.detail: apply_now maintained 91 events with +12.3% YoY growth despite 25.4% MoM decline, suggesting structural demand still outpaces seasonal volatility.

quick_wins.title: Geographic expansion emerging in key markets
quick_wins.detail: /new-york and /michigan/dundee location pages each achieved 25–28.6% engagement rates on minimal traffic, indicating strong intent in these untapped markets.

potential_warnings.title: Severe MoM traffic cliff
potential_warnings.detail: Sessions crashed 68.4% MoM to 10,994, with engaged sessions falling 60.0% to 865—a steeper decline than the 11.5% organic search drop, suggesting either campaign pause, seasonality, or attribution shift affecting direct traffic dominance (96.9%).

potential_warnings.title: High-value phone calls nearly extinct
potential_warnings.detail: Calls dropped to only 2 events (down 33.3% MoM, flat YoY), representing a critical funnel break for one of the highest-intent conversion types in auto lending.

potential_warnings.title: Outbound clicks declining YoY despite engagement
potential_warnings.detail: outbound_clicks fell 54.7% YoY to 254 events, suggesting either reduced third-party routing or shifted user behavior away from external partner sites.

potential_warnings.title: Engagement rate compression YoY
potential_warnings.detail: Engagement rate fell 47.2 pp YoY despite +136.3% growth in engaged session count, indicating Q1 2026 traffic surge brought lower-intent visitors to the site.

biggest_opportunities.title: Recover direct traffic baseline
biggest_opportunities.detail: Direct traffic (96.9% of sessions) plummeted 68.9% MoM despite +2803.5% YoY growth—investigate whether January/February campaign lift has subsided or if tracking/UTM implementation changed; reactivating or clarifying attribution could recapture thousands of sessions.

biggest_opportunities.title: Resuscitate phone call pathway
biggest_opportunities.detail: Only 2 calls in 30 days represents massive untapped funnel potential; audit call-to-action placement on homepage (/), review call tracking implementation, and test prominent phone number CTAs above the fold to drive high-intent conversions.

biggest_opportunities.title: Scale underperforming location pages
biggest_opportunities.detail: Geographic pages like /maryland/easton and /massachusetts/pittsfield show 0% engagement; analyze why /new-york and /michigan/dundee achieved 25%+ engagement, then apply messaging/UX learnings to similar location clusters to unlock localized demand.

biggest_opportunities.title: Capitalize on navigational momentum
biggest_opportunities.detail: navigational_clicks jumped 89.7% MoM to 129 events—users are exploring; pair with retargeting or personalized apply_now prompts to convert exploratory behavior into high-value apply_now or call events.

last_calendar_month.summary: March 2026 traffic declined sharply MoM (-61.0% sessions) but remains dramatically elevated YoY (+1824.0%), with YTD 2026 at 82,214 sessions vs. 2,199 in YTD 2025 (+3638.7%). The monthly trend shows a deceleration pattern: January 2026 peaked at 36,484 sessions, February held at 32,897, and March dropped to 12,833—suggesting either seasonal pullback, campaign wind-down, or market saturation. Engagement rate improved 1.5 percentage points MoM to 7.6%, though it lags YoY by 47.7 pp, indicating traffic quality is recovering relative to volume but remains below prior-year standards. Direct traffic dominates at 97.2% of sessions (12,473) but fell 61.6% MoM, while organic search remained stable MoM (-2.7%) and flat YoY (-9.4%). High-value conversion events are concerning: calls dropped 33.3% MoM to just 2 events, and apply_now declined 19.1% MoM to 93 events, despite healthy YoY growth of +13.4% in apply_now. Navigational clicks surged 130.5% MoM, suggesting users are exploring site structure but not converting; outbound_clicks held steady MoM (+1.6%) but fell 56.0% YoY. Homepage (/) remains the primary engagement driver at 38.6% engagement rate and 407 sessions, growing 6.3% MoM. YTD trajectory is strong, but March's sharp sequential decline and weak call volume warrant immediate investigation into channel health and conversion funnel optimization.

quick_wins.title: Homepage engagement is stable and outperforming
quick_wins.detail: The / landing page maintains 38.6% engagement rate (157 engaged of 407 sessions) and grew 6.3% MoM—the strongest performer by far among all pages.

quick_wins.title: Apply_now event shows YoY momentum despite MoM dip
quick_wins.detail: Apply_now totaled 93 events with +13.4% YoY growth, signaling underlying user intent; the 19.1% MoM decline may be temporary and reversible with minor channel or messaging optimization.

quick_wins.title: Organic search is stable and efficient
quick_wins.detail: Organic search held 252 sessions (2.0% of total) with only -2.7% MoM and -9.4% YoY change, indicating reliable baseline traffic and low churn risk for this channel.

potential_warnings.title: Dramatic MoM session collapse signals upstream problem
potential_warnings.detail: Sessions plummeted 61.0% from February to March (32,897 → 12,833), with direct traffic dropping 61.6% MoM. This sharp decline suggests a campaign pause, budget cut, or major channel disruption that requires immediate root-cause analysis.

potential_warnings.title: Phone calls have nearly disappeared
potential_warnings.detail: Only 2 call events in March, down 33.3% MoM and unchanged YoY—this is a critical conversion signal. Combined with the apply_now decline, it indicates the funnel is leaking at high-intent stages.

potential_warnings.title: Engagement quality remains far below prior-year baseline
potential_warnings.detail: Engagement rate is 47.7 pp lower YoY (7.6% vs. 55.3% implied), meaning the traffic influx is substantially lower-intent despite high volume gains. This suggests audience composition has shifted negatively or targeting accuracy has degraded.

potential_warnings.title: Navigational clicks surging while conversion stalls
potential_warnings.detail: Navigational clicks jumped 130.5% MoM and +70.0% YoY to 136 events, but high-value events (calls, apply_now) are declining—users are clicking through pages without converting.

potential_warnings.title: Location-specific pages show zero engagement
potential_warnings.detail: Multiple location pages (Durham NC, Greenfield MA, Willow Springs MO, Aurora IL, Clarksville MD) are attracting 7–9 sessions each with 0% engagement, indicating either poor landing page relevance or mismatched user intent.

biggest_opportunities.title: Investigate and revive the direct traffic channel
biggest_opportunities.detail: Direct traffic represents 97.2% of sessions but fell 61.6% MoM—this channel is the lifeblood of the site. Audit email campaigns, branded search, and bookmark/repeat visitor flows to identify the collapse point and recover volume.

biggest_opportunities.title: Optimize location landing pages for engagement
biggest_opportunities.detail: State and city-level pages (NC, MA, MO, IL, MD) are capturing traffic but zero conversions, suggesting poor page design, weak CTAs, or mismatched audience. A/B test messaging, add prominent call/apply buttons, and evaluate whether these pages should exist or redirect to homepage.

biggest_opportunities.title: Rebuild call volume through funnel optimization
biggest_opportunities.detail: With only 2 calls in March, audit the call-initiation path on homepage and apply_now pages. Add click-to-call buttons above the fold, simplify phone contact forms, and test SMS scheduling as an alternative high-intent conversion event.

biggest_opportunities.title: Reduce navigational friction and increase apply_now prominence
biggest_opportunities.detail: Users are clicking internal navigation (+130.5% MoM) but not applying; this suggests unclear CTA hierarchy. Consolidate navigation, make apply_now more discoverable on key pages, and test exit-intent offers to capture users exploring without converting.
